QF.id: IV Introduction to Interest Rate Derivatives_13

question.text: A call swaption gives the holder the right to:

answer.text: Enter a swap as a fixed rate payer
Enter a swap as a fixed rate receiver
Buy a bond at a predetermined price
Sell a bond at a predetermined price

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** A call swaption (also called a receiver swaption) gives the holder the right to enter an interest rate swap where they receive a fixed rate and pay a floating rate. A put swaption (payer swaption) gives the right to pay fixed and receive floating.
**The Trap:** This is counterintuitive — a call swaption is about receiving fixed, not paying. Think of it as buying the right to 'call' (receive) fixed cash flows.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Swaptions are tested in Series IV under OTC interest rate derivative products. They combine features of options and swaps.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A receiver swaption profits when interest rates fall (the fixed rate received becomes valuable). A payer swaption profits when rates rise. They're used by treasuries for contingent hedging.
